title: ficus caffra
intro: Ficus caffra, also known as the bushwillow tree, is a plant species native to southern Africa that belongs to the fig family (Moraceae). The bark of Ficus caffra has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating pain. In traditional herbal medicine, Ficus caffra is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion, or used as a tincture, to harness its medicinal properties.
